name: Thorn Ironfist
desc: A grizzled dwarven veteran from the Sky Citadel of Janderhoff. Stoic, dependable, and lethal with an axe.
roleTip: Front-line tank. High AC and HP. Use Power Attack for big damage.
personality: Stoic and few-worded, but fiercely loyal to those who earn his trust. Measures people by their deeds, not their words. Enjoys a good ale and hates goblins with a quiet, burning intensity.
appearance: Broad and stocky even for a dwarf, with granite-gray skin weathered by decades underground and on the surface. A thick braided beard streaked with iron-gray, deep-set amber eyes, and a prominent scar across the bridge of his nose from an orc's axe.
backstory: Thorn served forty years as a tunnel warden in Janderhoff's deepest galleries, holding the line against Darklands incursions. When a duergar raid killed his shield-brother Korgan, Thorn swore a blood-oath to carry Korgan's axe until he'd avenged the fallen. He left the Sky Citadel for the surface, following rumors of dark forces stirring along the Lost Coast. He tells himself it's about the oath, but the truth is simpler — the empty tunnels reminded him too much of what he'd lost.

name: Lyria Shadowstep
desc: A quick-witted elven rogue from the Mierani Forest who wandered south to Varisia. Picks locks, disarms traps, strikes from shadows.
roleTip: Scout & skill monkey. Flank enemies for Sneak Attack damage.
personality: Sharp-tongued and quick to smile, with a restless curiosity that gets her into trouble. Hates bullies and slavers. Keeps her real feelings buried under layers of sarcasm and deflection.
appearance: Tall and willowy with pale silver hair cropped short, bright green eyes that miss nothing, and nimble fingers always in motion. Wears dark leathers with Varisian embroidery, and keeps a thin scar along her jawline as a reminder of the first trap she failed to disarm.
backstory: Lyria grew up among the reclusive elves of the Mierani Forest, but the slow centuries of elven life suffocated her. She slipped away one moonless night, drawn south by Varisian caravan songs she'd heard drifting through the trees. In Magnimar she fell in with a thieves' guild, learning lockpicking and bladework in the city's underbelly. When the guild turned to darker work — kidnapping and extortion — she walked away, taking only her tools and a stolen rapier. She drifted to the Lost Coast, looking for honest trouble.

name: Aldric the Wise
desc: A scholarly Chelaxian wizard from Magnimar, devoted to studying the Thassilonian ruins that dot the Varisian landscape.
roleTip: Arcane blaster & utility. Sleep, Grease, Color Spray, Magic Missile.
personality: Methodical and endlessly curious, with a dry wit that surfaces at the worst moments. Treats every ancient inscription like a personal challenge. Impatient with superstition but fascinated by genuine mysteries.
appearance: Lean and pale-skinned with sharp Chelaxian features — high cheekbones, dark eyes, and ink-stained fingers. Keeps his black hair tied back severely. Wears practical traveling robes over leather, with a well-worn spellbook chained to his belt.
backstory: Born to a minor Chelaxian noble family that relocated to Magnimar, Aldric showed prodigious arcane talent from childhood. He apprenticed at the Stone of the Seers, where he became obsessed with Thassilonian magic — the ancient empire that once ruled Varisia. His mentor warned him that Thassilon's power was built on sin magic, but Aldric believed understanding darkness was the only way to fight it. When a colleague vanished investigating a ruin near Sandpoint, Aldric packed his spellbook and headed north, driven by equal parts concern and scholarly greed.

name: Sister Maren
desc: A compassionate Varisian healer raised in the Sandpoint Cathedral. Wields divine magic to mend wounds and smite undead.
roleTip: Healer & support. Channel Energy for group heals. Cast Bless for buffs.
personality: Warm and steadfast, with a quiet inner strength forged by loss. Sees the divine in small kindnesses — a shared meal, a bandaged wound. Patient with the frightened but fierce when the innocent are threatened.
appearance: A young Varisian woman with olive skin, dark wavy hair worn loose past her shoulders, and warm brown eyes. A silver Desna butterfly pendant hangs at her throat. Her hands are calloused from years of tending the sick, and faded Varisian tattoos trace her forearms.
backstory: Maren was orphaned during the Late Unpleasantness that rocked Sandpoint — the fire that consumed the old chapel killed her parents, both acolytes of Desna. Father Zantus took her in and raised her in the new cathedral, where she found solace in prayer and healing. She became the town's most trusted healer, tending fishermen's broken bones and children's fevers alike. But lately her dreams have turned dark — visions of stone faces screaming underground — and she believes Desna is calling her beyond Sandpoint's walls.

name: Kaelen Stormblade
desc: A half-elf ranger born of Varisian and elven parents. Patrols the wild borderlands and hunts goblinoids along the Lost Coast.
roleTip: Ranged DPS & tracker. Favored Enemy gives combat bonuses.
personality: Quiet and watchful, most comfortable with miles of wilderness between him and the nearest town. Speaks plainly when he speaks at all. Carries a deep-seated need to prove he belongs somewhere — too elven for the Varisians, too human for the elves.
appearance: Lean and rangy with sun-darkened skin, angular half-elven features, and storm-gray eyes that earned him his name. Keeps his light brown hair tied in a rough knot. Wears well-worn studded leather covered in forest stains, with a Varisian scarf wrapped around one arm — a gift from his mother.
backstory: Born to a Varisian caravan dancer and an elven wanderer who vanished before his birth, Kaelen grew up on the road between Magnimar and Riddleport. He never fit the caravan life — too quiet, too restless — and at fourteen he walked into the Churlwood alone and didn't come back for three years. He emerged a tracker, fluent in goblin sign-markings and the language of broken branches. The Sandpoint militia hired him to scout the goblin tribes of the hinterlands, and he's been patrolling the Lost Coast ever since, trading goblin ears for bounties and sleeping under the stars.

name: Grakthar the Unbroken
desc: A towering half-orc barbarian from the Hold of Belkzen, scarred by countless battles. Left the orc clans to find his own path.
roleTip: Melee powerhouse. Rage for +4 STR/CON. Greatsword + Power Attack.
personality: Blunt, direct, and prone to solving problems with violence — but not stupid. Has a dry, dark humor and an unexpected code of honor: he won't strike the helpless or fight the outnumbered. Struggles with the rage inside him, unsure whether it's a gift or a curse.
appearance: Massive even by half-orc standards, standing nearly seven feet with gray-green skin covered in ritual scarring. His jaw juts forward with prominent tusks, one chipped from a headbutt. Yellow eyes burn beneath a heavy brow. Wears battered hide armor decorated with trophies — teeth, claws, and a single tarnished medal from a forgotten battle.
backstory: Grakthar was born in the Blood Trail clan of Belkzen, where weakness meant death. He survived his youth through brute strength and an iron will, earning the name 'Unbroken' after enduring the clan's coming-of-age trial — three days chained to a post in orc territory, fighting off anything that came for him. But when the clan chieftain ordered a raid on a Lastwall frontier village, Grakthar saw children in the burning houses and couldn't swing his sword. He killed two clansmen who tried to stop him from pulling survivors free, then fled south with a death mark on his head. Now he wanders Varisia, selling his sword to anyone who isn't asking him to butcher the defenseless.

name: Seraphine Dawnlight
desc: A radiant Taldan paladin trained in Lastwall, sworn to vanquish evil and protect the innocent from the horrors of Belkzen and Ustalav.
roleTip: Tank & off-healer. Smite Evil for huge damage. Lay on Hands for heals.
personality: Disciplined and principled, with a commanding presence that inspires confidence. Holds herself to an impossibly high standard and struggles when the world proves morally gray. Beneath the armor and oaths, she's quietly terrified of failing the people who depend on her.
appearance: Tall and broad-shouldered with golden-brown Taldan skin, high cheekbones, and striking amber eyes. Her dark hair is cut short and practical beneath her helm. A sunburst of Iomedae is engraved into her shield and tattooed on her sword arm. She carries herself with military bearing, every movement precise and deliberate.
backstory: Seraphine was raised in Vigil, the fortress-city of Lastwall, where her family served as minor officers in the crusade against the Whispering Tyrant's lingering influence. She was consecrated to Iomedae at birth and trained from childhood in blade and prayer. Her first patrol along the border of Belkzen ended in a massacre — her unit was ambushed by an orc war party, and she was the only survivor, saved by a burst of divine light she couldn't explain. Her superiors called it a sign from Iomedae and sent her south to Varisia, following reports of undead stirring in Thassilonian ruins. She accepted the mission, but privately she wonders if the goddess saved her — or if she simply wasn't brave enough to die with her comrades.

name: Bramble Thornwick
desc: A flamboyant gnome bard from Brastlewark whose songs inspire courage and dread. Left Cheliax seeking stories worth singing about.
roleTip: Buffer & face. Inspire Courage boosts the whole party.
personality: Irrepressibly cheerful, theatrical, and prone to narrating events as they happen in rhyming couplets. Collects stories the way others collect coins — always hungry for a new tale. Hides a genuine fear of the Bleaching, the gnomish curse of boredom that slowly drains color and life from unstimulated gnomes.
appearance: Small even for a gnome, barely three feet tall, with wild magenta hair that defies gravity and shifts hue with his mood. Bright violet eyes sparkle with mischief above a pointed nose. Wears a patchwork coat of colorful fabrics collected from a dozen nations, with tiny bells sewn into the hem that jingle when he walks. His lute is battered but lovingly maintained, covered in carved runes.
backstory: Bramble was born in Brastlewark, the gnome capital hidden within Cheliax, where the Thrune government tolerated gnomes as useful eccentrics. He trained as a court entertainer, singing for Chelish nobles who found his antics amusing. But when he witnessed a Hellknight execute a halfling slave for singing a forbidden song, something broke in him. He stole a horse (well, a pony), his master's best lute, and every coin he could grab, then fled north through Nidal and into Varisia. Now he wanders the Lost Coast, collecting songs and stories to fight back the Bleaching — and secretly composing an epic ballad about Chelish tyranny that he hopes will one day spark a revolution.

name: Whisper
desc: A mysterious halfling sorcerer from Andoran with draconic blood in her veins. Freed from bondage, she now wanders in search of the truth behind her bloodline.
roleTip: Spontaneous caster. No spellbook needed. Draconic bloodline powers.
personality: Guarded and soft-spoken — hence the name — but observant, absorbing everything around her with quiet intensity. Rarely volunteers information about her past. Has a fierce independent streak born from years of captivity, and a surprisingly vicious temper when someone tries to control her.
appearance: Tiny even for a halfling, with dusky brown skin, large golden-amber eyes with vertically slit pupils that betray her draconic heritage, and short dark hair with a single streak of copper. Faint scale-like patterns shimmer along her forearms when she channels magic. Dresses in simple, practical clothes — she owns little and keeps it that way.
backstory: Whisper — she's never shared her birth name — was born into halfling servitude in Cheliax, property of a minor noble house. Her draconic bloodline manifested at age twelve when she accidentally set fire to her master's library during a beating. The Chelish authorities wanted her destroyed; instead, a passing Bellflower Network agent smuggled her north to Andoran, where she was freed under the People's Council. She spent years learning to control her power from a retired sorcerer in Almas, but the old man died before explaining where her bloodline came from. Now she follows the thread of her heritage north into Varisia, where ancient dragons once served the Thassilonian runelords.

name: Oakenheart
desc: A weathered Shoanti druid of the Shriikirri-Quah (Hawk Clan) who communes with the ancient spirits of the Storval Plateau.
roleTip: Versatile caster & summoner. Wild Shape into beasts at level 4.
personality: Speaks in deliberate, measured phrases, often using nature metaphors that confuse city folk. Deeply spiritual but not preachy — he lives his beliefs rather than proclaiming them. Uncomfortable in buildings and crowds, but fiercely protective of the natural order and the people who respect it.
appearance: A powerfully built Shoanti man in his late thirties, with copper-brown skin, a shaved head bearing elaborate blue-green clan tattoos of the Shriikirri-Quah, and sharp dark eyes that seem to track movement like a hawk's. Wears weathered hide armor decorated with feathers and bone. A living oak leaf is always tucked behind his ear, impossibly green regardless of season.
backstory: Oakenheart — born Krojun Eats-Fire — was raised on the Storval Plateau among the Shriikirri-Quah, the Hawk Clan of the Shoanti people. The clan elders recognized his bond with the land spirits when, as a boy, he calmed a wildfire by speaking to it. They sent him to train with a druid circle in the Mindspin Mountains, where he spent a decade learning the old ways. He returned to find his clan diminished — lowlander settlers had fouled the sacred springs, and a plague was killing the thunderhawks his people revered. The elders named him Oakenheart for his unyielding resolve and sent him south to learn what poison the lowlanders had brought. He travels with his wolf companion Ashfang, following the corruption downstream toward the Lost Coast.
